title: Visitability standards — original framework
note: Org website confirmed real. Concrete Change is the established US visitability advocacy org founded by Eleanor Smith. URL-confirmed.

authors: Antonakos, C., Giordani, B. & Ashton-Miller, J.
title: Wayfinding With Visuo-Spatial Impairment from Stroke and Traumatic Brain Injury
note: Confirmed. Open-access journal. Exploratory qualitative study with 4 participants.

title: DeafSpace SLCC rounded corners self-report
note: Internal report — no public URL or publication record found. BPC description "SLCC rounded corners self-report" is too vague to verify. Forward to BPC author.

title: DeafSpace Design Guidelines (working draft, 85pp)
note: Known grey literature document. DeafSpace Design Guidelines working draft confirmed circulated by Gallaudet. Not publicly archived but referenced widely in DeafSpace literature.

title: DeafSpace Design Guidelines Volume 1
note: Confirmed. Gallaudet University Press published DeafSpace Design Guidelines. ISBN-level confirmation not retrieved but publication is real and widely cited.

title: Deaf Space in Adamorobe: An Ethnography in a Village in Ghana
note: Confirmed. Gallaudet UP confirmed publisher. ISBN found in catalog.

title: ESKEH accessibility survey method (ESKEH-kartoitusmenetelmä)
publisher: Invalidiliitto (Finnish Association of People with Physical Disabilities)
note: Confirmed. ESKEH is a validated Finnish accessibility audit framework published by Invalidiliitto. URL confirmed.

title: DeafScape: Applying DeafSpace to Landscape
note: Confirmed. Published UC Berkeley student journal Ground Up Journal, Issue 7, May 2018. Author Alexa Vaughn is a Deaf landscape architect. Widely cited in DeafSpace/landscape design literature.

title: Enquête Ifop/APF — survey of disabled persons in built environment France
note: Ifop/APF survey. APF France Handicap is a real organisation. Specific 2020 survey not confirmed. Holding for second search.

title: Patient perspectives on outpatient mental health environment (n=13)
note: Internal pilot study. No publication record found. BPC "Permanente/Kaiser San Jose 2020" is too vague — may be internal quality improvement report. Forward to BPC author for source.

title: Against Access (essay)
publisher: Published in McSweeney's Issue 61 (fall 2022); republished as chapter in Touch the Future: A Manifesto in Essays (W.W. Norton, 2023)
note: BPC lists "Against Access McSweeney's 2021" — YEAR WRONG (published 2022 in McSweeney's), and it is an essay not a book. No standalone 2021 Clark book exists. Correct citation is the McSweeney's essay OR the Touch the Future chapter. YEAR AND FORMAT CORRECTION REQUIRED.

title: Accessible Home Design, 2nd Edition
note: Confirmed. PVA is a major US disability org. Accessible Home Design is their established publication. 2nd edition confirmed real.

authors: Dementia Services Development Centre (DSDC), University of Stirling
title: Environment Assessment Dementia Design Audit Tool (EADDAT)
note: Confirmed. DSDC Stirling is the lead international centre for dementia design. EADDAT is their validated audit tool. URL confirmed. BPC has two entries (13 and 14) for same tool — duplicate; one should be removed.

title: Autistic-led assessment of NHS CAMHS inpatient environments
note: NDTi is a real UK organisation. 2022 autistic-led CAMHS environment assessment not confirmed in 1 search. Holding for second search.

title: Touch the Future: A Manifesto in Essays
note: Confirmed. Published Norton 2023. Contains "Against Access" and other essays on DeafBlind experience and Protactile.

authors: SCIE (Social Care Institute for Excellence) / Habinteg
title: Habinteg Housing Association case study — wheelchair-accessible bungalow: ADL independence documented
note: SCIE is a UK government-funded social care knowledge organisation. Habinteg is UK's leading accessible housing provider. Case study confirmed real. URL-confirmed.

authors: Wilson, R.L., Hutton, A., & Foureur, M.
title: Promoting mental health recovery by design: Physical, procedural, and relational security in the context of the mental health built environment
journal: International Journal of Mental Health Nursing
note: Confirmed. Qualitative descriptive study, Australia, n=38 (inpatients, carers, staff).

authors: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
title: ME/CFS: Clinical Care for Severely Affected Patients
note: Confirmed. CDC ME/CFS clinical care guidelines. URL confirmed. BPC has 3 entries for the same CDC source (entries 19, 20, 21) — all point to same URL. Two duplicates should be removed or consolidated.

title: 障害者の居住にも対応した住宅の設計ハンドブック (Housing Design Handbook for Persons with Disabilities)
note: Japanese-language source. DPI Japan is a real organisation. Specific 2024 handbook not confirmed via English-language search. Requires Japanese-language search or BPC author confirmation.

title: Inclusive Housing Design Guide (Habinteg, CAE, RIBA)
note: DOI confirmed. Published Routledge 2024. Co-produced by Habinteg, Centre for Accessible Environments (CAE), RIBA. RCOTSS-Housing OT contribution confirmed.

title: ME/CFS: Clinical Care for Healthcare Providers
note: Confirmed. Bateman Horne Center is a leading US ME/CFS clinical and research centre. Clinical care guidance URL confirmed. BPC has 2 entries (24, 25) for same source — duplicate.

authors: Nuccio, J., granda, l., Clark, J.L. et al.
title: Protactile language and movement (Wikipedia article with cited academic sources)
note: BPC cites "Wikipedia/Nuccio, granda, Clark" for Protactile. The Wikipedia article is real and cites academic sources. For publication-grade purposes, BPC should cite the primary academic sources not Wikipedia. Flag for BPC upgrade to Nuccio et al. peer-reviewed papers on Protactile linguistics.

title: DIY sensory corners in Nigerian homes (foam mats, neutral paint)
note: BPC: "(Nigeria Co-1 source) n.d." — No author, no date, no source URL. Unverifiable. CLOSED-DELETED.

title: Lived experience research and resources
note: DiMHN is a real UK professional network for mental health design. URL confirmed. BPC description "lived experience research" is general — specific publications at dimhn.org should be cited directly if used.

title: De Hogeweyk post-occupancy evaluation (Vivium Zorggroep / BuroKade)
note: BPC itself marks as [UNVERIFIABLE — internal document; background evidence only]. CLOSED-DELETED per project rules. Not citable.
